title: Walls and Gates
prompt:
You are given a 2D grid where:
- -1 represents a wall,
- 0 represents a gate,
- 2147483647 represents an empty room.

Fill each empty room with the distance to its nearest gate. If it is impossible to reach a gate, leave it as 2147483647.
Return the modified grid.
constraintQuestions: Are moves only up, right, down, left? (Yes.)
Should we return the same grid after modification? (Yes.)
What should we do with unreachable rooms? (Leave as 2147483647.)
Is an empty grid valid? (Yes, return as-is.)

walkthrough.title: Multi-source BFS from gates
walkthrough.body:
Start BFS from all gates at once.
Each BFS layer increases distance by 1.
When you reach an empty room with INF, set its distance and enqueue it.
Walls are skipped.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#
Python

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(r*c) because each cell is processed at most once.
Space: O(r*c) for the queue in the worst case.
